chapter_context.summary: Elijah is taken up to heaven in a fiery whirlwind; Elisha receives a double portion of his spirit, parts the Jordan, purifies Jericho's bad water, and curses mocking youths who are mauled by bears.

outline.text: Elijah and Elisha's Final Journey from Gilgal to Jordan
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elijah and Elisha's Final Journey from Gilgal to Jordan (2:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Elijah Taken up to Heaven in a Whirlwind; Elisha Receives Double Portion
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elijah Taken up to Heaven in a Whirlwind; Elisha Receives Double Portion (2:9-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Elisha Smites Jordan; Prophets Search for Elijah in Vain
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elisha Smites Jordan; Prophets Search for Elijah in Vain (2:13-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Elisha Heals the Waters of Jericho with Salt
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elisha Heals the Waters of Jericho with Salt (2:19-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Irreverent Youths Mock Elisha and are Torn by Two Bears
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Irreverent Youths Mock Elisha and are Torn by Two Bears (2:23-25), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

key_themes.title: Prophetic Authority
key_themes.description: Elisha carries the mantle of Elijah, showing that God's word stands firm in judgment and mercy regardless of political power.

key_themes.title: Covenant Faithfulness and Exile
key_themes.description: The tragic fall of Samaria and Jerusalem shows that persistent rebellion and idolatry inevitably lead to judgment.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 2 prompt you to trust in God's promises even during seasons of spiritual decline?
What warnings or instructions does this chapter hold for guarding your own heart against syncretism?

cultural_historical.title: The Assyrian and Babylonian Empires
cultural_historical.text: The rise of Mesopotamian super-empires (Assyria and Babylon) serves as the historical backdrop for the exile and division of the Hebrew kingdoms.
